---
title: "Ian Gault"
subtitle: "Data Scientist • Professional Biologist"
toc: true
toc-title: "On this page"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: media/109_IanG_2024.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/iangault
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/iangault
---

## About

Master of Data Science candidate (UBC, graduating Jun 2026) and Registered Professional Biologist with six years of experience designing reproducible data systems for biological and chemical datasets. Graduate research in wet-lab spans mass spectrometry, chemical extraction, cell cultures, and endocrine disruption & high-throughput cytotoxicity assays; subsequent work & training in end-to-end ML pipelines, ETL workflows, and data modelling. Expertise in identifying inefficiencies, building datasets from legacy sources, and translating between multi-disciplinary teams to support defensible, data-driven decisions.

## Education

**Master of Data Science** *(in progress, Jun 2026)*
University of British Columbia, Vancouver, BC

**Certificate — Applied Data Science: Leveraging AI for Decision-making** *(2023)*
Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, MA

**Master of Science — Analytical & Environmental Toxicology** *(2015–2019)*
University of Alberta, Edmonton, AB

**Bachelor of Science — Biological Science & Science Psychology** *(with Distinction, 2009–2014)*
University of Alberta, Edmonton, AB

## Experience

**Environmental Scientist** | WSP | Jul 2022 – Aug 2025
Designed data science pipelines in R for environmental monitoring studies; ETL workflows, legacy data harmonization, and modelling. Lead data scientist, scientific communicator, and project coordinator for 10+ clients over three years.

**Environmental Specialist** | Hatfield Consultants | May 2019 – Jun 2022
Led toxicological risk studies and coordinated large-scale field programs and end-to-end data pipelines — from field sample collection to R-based ETL outputs — for 10+ clients.

**Graduate Research Assistant** | University of Alberta | 2015–2019
Used cell cultures, molecular techniques (RTCA, YES/YAS), and Orbitrap mass spectrometry to perform toxicity testing and chemical characterization of oil sands process-affected water.

## Skills

**Data & Programming:** R, Python, SQL, modular pipelines, ETL workflows

**Machine Learning:** PCA, classification, regression, clustering, neural networks

**Statistics:** Descriptive, inferential, Bayesian, causal; temporospatial analysis

**Wet Lab:** Mass spectrometry, cell cultures, molecular techniques, field sampling

**Tools:** Git, GitHub, Make, Bash, Docker, Quarto, R Markdown, Shiny, dashboarding

**Environmental Scientist** | WSP | Jul 2022 – Aug 2025
Lead data scientist and project coordinator across 10+ environmental monitoring clients over three years. Designed R-based ETL workflows, harmonized legacy datasets, and built reproducible modelling pipelines for water quality, sediment, and contaminated sites work. Developed tools that automated reporting and secured repeated contract renewals.

**Environmental Specialist** | Hatfield Consultants | May 2019 – Jun 2022
Led toxicological risk studies and coordinated large-scale field programs end-to-end — from sample collection and laboratory communications to R-based data pipelines and regulatory reporting — for 10+ clients.

**Graduate Research Assistant** | University of Alberta | 2015–2019
Conducted toxicity testing and non-targeted chemical characterization of oil sands process-affected water using Orbitrap mass spectrometry, cell cultures, and molecular bioassays. Published in *ACS EST Water*.

## Skills

**Programming & Data:** R, Python, SQL · ETL pipelines · reproducible workflows · legacy data harmonization

**Machine Learning & Statistics:** Classification, regression, clustering, PCA, neural networks · Bayesian, causal, and spatiotemporal methods

**Infrastructure & Tools:** Git/GitHub, Docker, Make, Bash · Quarto, R Markdown, Shiny · dashboarding (Shiny, Plotly, DT)

**Scientific Communication:** Technical reporting for regulatory and non-technical audiences · data visualization for decision-making · peer-reviewed publication · conference presentation

**Project & Team Leadership:** Multi-client project coordination · study design · cross-functional team leadership (biologists, GIS specialists, project managers) · budget and timeline management

**Domain Knowledge:** Environmental chemistry & toxicology · regulatory frameworks (provincial & federal) · wet lab methods · field sampling & biomonitoring


## Designations

- **Registered Professional Biologist (RPBio)** — College of Applied Biologists, BC (2024–present)

### Professional Development

**AI-Assisted Development: Practices and Pitfalls** *(May 2026)*
UBC Master of Data Science — one-day workshop on agentic coding practices

**Upper Bound Conference** *(2024)*
Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute (AMII)

**Dry It Up with R** *(2022)*
Vancouver DataJam - workshop on techniques used to "Don't Repeat Yourself" in R

### Academic Awards

| Year | Award |
|------|-------|
| 2017 | Queen Elizabeth II Graduate Scholarship |
| 2016 | Medical Sciences Graduate Program Scholarship |
| 2016 | Faculty of Medicine & Dentistry 75th Anniversary Award |
| 2016 | Queen Elizabeth II Graduate Scholarship |
| 2015 | Medical Sciences Graduate Program Scholarship |
| 2015 | DynaLIFEDX Major Incentive Award |
| 2014 | Individual Education Abroad Scholarship |
| 2010, 2012, 2014 | Jason Lang Scholarship |

### Community Service

**Standards & Guidance Committee** — BC College of Oral Health Professionals *(Apr 2025 – Apr 2026)*
Served as a public member reviewing professional standards and guidance during the consolidation of multiple health professions under a unified regulatory body; engaged with governance structures, working groups of subject-matter experts, and the rollout of new enforceable professional standards.

**Board Member (Secretary) & Mental Health Team Lead** — Edmonton Men's Health Collective *(2015–2019)*
Executive board member of a grassroots collective improving health outcomes for sexual and gender minority men. Provided strategic oversight for mental health initiatives; supported grant proposals; bridged community members with stakeholders and subject-matter experts. Designed and led a 6-month harm reduction campaign: coordinated 5 naloxone training sessions reaching ~300 community members and produced an interview-based social media series featuring people with lived experience.

**Inclusive Health Conference** — Sexuality & Gender Advocacy Committee, Edmonton *(May 2017)*
Presented as EMHC representative on mental health outcomes and LGBTQ+ issues in healthcare and medical education.

**Treasurer, LMP Student Group** — University of Alberta *(2016–2019)*
Managed budget and provided input on departmental policies for the Laboratory Medicine & Pathology student group.
